metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_247
metadata.comment:
Rationale: MADD/DENN was shown to function as KIF1 motor adapter. GTP-bound Rab3, which is a marker of synaptic vesicle transport vesicles (STVs), binds MADD which then allows KIF1-dependent transport of Rab3-STVs. Expression of a dominant negative MADD (death domain) blocks binding of Rab3-STVs to KIF1. Reduced Rab3-STVs in distal axons and a decrease in anterograde-moving Rab3-STVs in proximal axons were observed in neurons expressing dominant negative MADD/DENN or miRNA against MADD/DENN. Taken together, these results indicates that movement of STVs to presynaptic sites via KIF1 motors are dependent on MADD/DENN.

29/3/2017 Pim
- Rab3A is a known SV protein and in this paper and PMID:7539720 considered as a marker of precursor synaptic vesicles (see discussion with expert in feedback section). Effects on Rab3A lcoalization are therefore annotated against transport of synaptic vesicles.. Experimental description: Tissue: mouse
From Materials and Methods "Rab3 isoforms and DENN/MADD cDNA were amplified by PCR from RIKEN FANTOM mouse clones (Dnaform) and from KIAA0358 clone (Kazusa DNA Institute), respectively."
